column23: Comments
I got Cap and SL rares at the side1, it was a breeze from there.
The first game went pretty rough hitting scheme and master strikes very early. The 2nd game we were able to stabilize much quicker and recruit cards that worked together generally well.
Scheme makes this luck based, if you get Black widow card you need, or will twist eat it
At first glance I thought Thanos would destroy me. Unlike Avengers Infinity War, Star-Lord did actually defeat Thanos this time. Probably because Gamora was alive and well now.

Sometimes it is too easy to forget that with this set up you have to limit your purchases. I was lucky the last scheme twists were at the bottom as I had only one card left in the hero deck. Luckily Groot's Prune the Growth really helped whittle decks down.

Not fun. I feel like both hands had good things going in both tries but happened to draw most of the scheme twists pretty early in the deck. Pure time schemes are not fun in a tournament setting because there is nothing the play can do to mitigate an early flood.
So hard, The Mad Titan is unstoppable
Super-Adaptoid stood no chance!
X-force wolverine/Elektra was surprisingly really strong! Shield team very medium, until it got quake's ult, but didn't really catch up. War machine was also there, Haha.

I chose the risky way by adding two heroes with only 3 strength cards but it paid off! Had several additional "undercover"-points in my second run and now I'm glad I saved Iceman or Forge for greater challenges.
Got lucky, three twists in the first 6 cards so our decks were not well built yet.
The three chosen heroes didn't have much if anything in common which made selecting another two heroes VERY challenging.
Chosen it to add Strength heroes, which made the end of the game a bit harrowing.
Bad team synergy
Interesting having to select Heroes not just based on which Heroes that were pre-selected but also having to keep Strength cards in mind for the Scheme

column2: Mephisto Pulls Reality into the Negative Zone
column23: It was a horrible game at the beginning - when I finally got 10 attack I drew an even twist from VD and all went to the gutter. Of course when I was getting better with recruit per turn I drew the next turn and it messed me up. Groot and Gamora were not popping off as often as expected to help me remedy the situation with shard gaining that could be used for either attack or recruit (thanks to Groot). The real MVPs were Human Torch and Iron Fist that generated enormous attack value and delivered the MM's all 5 blows right after twist 5 in 2-3 turns. I really hope I won't see that scheme anytime soon again lol

So many different costs made Iron Fist go nuts for WaspWoman, and Blade just loved taking villains out in the sewers and rooftops. So many shards were cached thanks to the Deadliest Woman in the Galaxy and saved until reality finally left the negative zone.
Shards were handy for nega-recruiting!

I probably wouldn't have done so well if it wasn't for the rocket and Gamora combo
I was able to stockpile my shards to take the mastermind out in one fell swoop

Thought things were going pretty well, especially after Mephisto's "Price of Failure" Tactic totally failed! The other side had just fought him for +2 with his Shards and cleared them, then the next draw was Captain Atlas who got no bonus Attack after we just cleared those Shards! Unfortunately both plays got down to the Final Blow on Twist 6, and the game ender came only one or two turns after.

With an early God(Devil)slayer Blade, the left was able to get multiple hits on our lovable demon. The negative zone proved to keep me off balance, however, and wasn’t able to get a win in either game. Should’ve gone all in on Versatile. 7th and final twist came all too early.
I was able to gain a lot of attack but only during the flipped twist. Good setup & was fun to play
Silly Odin kept filling the city with fodder for my Enchant the Senses/Kingpin engine. Once that got going it was game over for him!
The heroes used had few cards with high attack. This helped Abomination a lot. Got a rare right before Thor came in.
This scheme is not my favorite.
Rough. At least I hit Odin twice before I went insane...
Swingy Scheme! Even with non-randomized heroes on a third run I had no chance to win this! Bindings are always a pain!
Trapped in the Asylum is a tough scheme that really jumbles your ability to form great combos...it was fun though

column2: Madelyne Pryor's Secret Invasion of the Skrull Shapeshifters
column23: Widow v. Goblin Queen? Fish in a barrel.

Black Widow's perfect running mate is Apocalyptic Kitty Pride- they both get your deck down to nothing, and keep the HQ flowing. The otherside got to mop up all the Spectrum and different Team stuff, including acquiring 5/6 rares available by the end of the game!
Black Widow + Spider-Gwen bystander shenanigans. Need I say more?
My wife used Black Widow + Spider-Gwen as a great combo, and I made sure to throw in two more teams when picking Heroes, so my two captains dominated with all the variety!
Got Black widow's rare near the end, really helped finish off Madelyne

Time Traveling Jean Grey is no help against doppelgänger Madelyne Pryor. Worst hero ever. She spends the whole game rearranging the city and hoping to occasionally draw an extra card. It’s mostly just noise till Black Widow (who’s being fed bystanders constantly) shows up and 1 shots the mastermind.
first game went absolutely horribly, 2nd game came through to give me an acceptable submission
No

Went way better than expected. Ollie was able to thin his deck enough that once he could hit Annihilus, he consistently did so. Kylah racked up SHIELD level and dealt the last two hits in a single turn.

Despite having access to Dark City, I didn't see it quite working with the assigned 3 heroes. I picked Brainstorm to set up feasting and daredevil and that worked out pretty well. I picked ff4 to have something to counter the scheme with focus, that didn't come up very much. The cards never seemed to sync up right with the scheme twists. There was actually too much KO going on.
Mockingbird is way more dangerous than I originally thought.

The combination of Streets, Annihilation Wave, and SHIELD levels made for some very thin decks and the bribe mechanics from Streets and Joe Fixit was the key to winning.  MCU side landed the Grey Hulk's rare on turn 4 and used Stretching Credibility to get to it faster.  Comic side took two Threaten and Bribes and made sure Annihilus' momentum was as low as possible.  Final turn was comic side emptying both draw and discard pile, recruiting Super-Skrull's rare, and then using Blind Justice to guess 7 to get it and reach 14 attack to land the Final Blow.

First game I got a ton of Tech Shield heroes and had a nice engine going but couldn't get the final blow before the 10th Twist. Second game Deathlok and Rick didn't show up at all, but Daredevil and Super-Skrull had a really tight deck throwing lots of attack very quickly. Probably wouldn't try to chase the Shield combos if I played this over again.

column2: Mr. Sinister Smashes Two Dimensions Together
column23: Ranged deck on the left and strength deck on the right. Iceman obliterated Mr Sinister with 60 attack on a turn, drawing their entire deck, as well as all the new stuff they just bought!! Ridiculous!!

I always want the heroes I choose to have a reasonable impact on the game. Lady Thor did her job of supporting Captain Marvel but X-Force Wolverine is going to have back pain from how hard he carried this. He provided extra KO power to the Marvel/Lady Thor side while providing KO power, card drawn and significant attack to the other.
Wonderful Challenge!!!

Very challenging setup just couldn't get the decks to thin down enough or were too thinned down. However, it was a decent challenge and what didn't help were not the scheme twists it was the Master Strikes who kept making me discard for each bystander.
I really tried to get Colossus/Wolverine and Captain Marvel/Iceman to work but the shuffle in both decks rallied against me.

A DAY LIKE ANY OTHER! Player one really quickly get this rare and then just beating everything with Avengers cards from Lady Thor and Captain Marvel. Player two get Colossus and try to clean the city but getting lots of wounds. Master strike is painful thing from MS, and scheme twist - play two cards from villain deck was trouble as well. In the end the city was full and with Cap rare a generated enough attack to kill MS. Nice setup.
Trickier than it initially looked, we got hit really hard the first time but we're able to adjust our strategy the second time

column2: Immortal Emperor Zheng Zhu's Unbreakable Enigma Code
column23: Got to give Shang-Chi's rare to the other hand using Weasel at just the right time, as their deck was super thin, and5 twists had been played and the code cracked!! My kung fu is greater than yours Zheng Zhu!!

We enjoyed the challenge of this setup, but we despise this scheme lol. We chose Peter Parker/Aunt May and Shang Chi for two reasons (1) It eliminated Blue from the Enigma Code, and (2) Peter Parker and Spider-Gwen don’t rely on Spider-Friends team synergy. One player specifically focused on recruiting 2 cost cards. In Game 1, Shang Chi’s best card saved us big time. In Game 2, the Enigma Code destroyed us by claiming Hulking and Shang Chi’s best cards.

first game i went infinite with daredevil which felt amazing! although,it happened a little too late cuz i could hit the MM only for the final blow with it, both games ended up pretty much equal in scores so i hope i picked the right one for submitted results!
no
We had so many hands with enough to hit the mastermind and only got him once in both games due to poor luck (and a poor grasp on the rules of the scheme the first round).
The enigma code is a great thematic scheme that makes things feel like a cat-and-mouse game.
